It is classified within the order Asparagales and the class Liliopsida. The species Stigmatodactylus cymbalariifolius is also known as a type of leafless orchid. It is characterized by its unique leafless habit, which distinguishes it from other orchid species.

intro: Stigmatodactylus cymbalariifolius is a species of epiphytic orchid native to tropical regions of Asia, characterized by its unique, cymbal-shaped leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of S. cymbalariifolius include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, S. cymbalariifolius is often prepared as a decoction or infused in hot water to make a tea, or dried and ground into a powder for use in capsules or tinctures.
